635	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level charts and the glue data
636	
637	We assemble the affine charts (`affineChart`), their principal-open overlaps
638	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`, the transition isomorphisms (`transitionMap`), and
639	the cocycle (`cocycleCondition`) into the data of
640	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, whose `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme
641	`Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
642	
643	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
644	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
645	
646	/-- `P^I_I = 1`: the `I`-minor determinant of the universal matrix `X^I` is the
647	unit `1`, since `X^I_I` is the identity (`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`).
648	Project-local. -/
649	theorem minorDet_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
650	    minorDet d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
651	  rw [minorDet, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.det_one]
652	
653	/-- The principal-open overlap `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` as a scheme: the affine
654	spectrum of the away-localisation of the chart ring `R^I` at the minor determinant
655	`P^I_J`. Project-local: the `V`-object of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
656	noncomputable def chartOverlap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
657	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Scheme :=
658	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
659	
660	/-- The canonical open immersion `U^I_J → U^I` of the principal open into the chart,
661	the comorphism of the structure map `R^I → R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local: the `f`-field
662	of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
663	noncomputable def chartIncl (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
664	    (hJ : J.card = d) : chartOverlap d r I J hI hJ ⟶ affineChart d r I :=
665	  Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
666	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
667	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
668	
669	instance chartIncl_isOpenImmersion (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
670	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsOpenImmersion (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) :=
671	  inferInstanceAs (IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
672	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
673	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))))

674	
675	/-- The self-inclusion `U^I_I → U^I` is an isomorphism: since `P^I_I = 1`
676	(`minorDet_self`) the away-localisation is the identity, so its `Spec` is an iso.
677	Project-local: the `f_id`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
678	theorem chartIncl_self_isIso (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
679	    IsIso (chartIncl d r I I hI hI) := by
680	  have hx : IsUnit (minorDet d r I I hI hI) := by rw [minorDet_self]; exact isUnit_one
681	  have e : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ ≃ₐ[_]
682	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI) :=
683	    IsLocalization.atUnit _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) _ hx
684	  have hbij : Function.Bijective
685	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
686	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI))) := by
687	    have hfun : (⇑(algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
688	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))) = ⇑e := by
689	      funext y; simp [← e.commutes y]
690	    rw [hfun]; exact e.bijective
691	  have : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
692	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
693	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))) :=
694	    (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mpr hbij
695	  change IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
696	    (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
697	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))))
698	  infer_instance
699	
700	/-- The scheme-level transition `U^I_J → U^J_I`, the comorphism (`Spec.map`) of the
701	ring transition map `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local: the
702	`t`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
703	noncomputable def chartTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
704	    (hJ : J.card = d) : chartOverlap d r I J hI hJ ⟶ chartOverlap d r J I hJ hI :=
705	  Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ))
706	
707	/-- `t_{I,I} = id`: the self-transition is the identity, from `transitionMap_self`.
708	Project-local: the `t_id`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
709	theorem chartTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
710	    chartTransition d r I I hI hI
711	      = CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id (chartOverlap d r I I hI hI) := by
712	  rw [chartTransition, transitionMap_self, CommRingCat.ofHom_id, Spec.map_id]
713	  rfl
714	
715	/-- The pullback of two principal-open inclusions
716	`Spec R[1/x] → Spec R ← Spec R[1/y]` is `Spec R[1/(xy)]`: combine `pullbackSpecIso`
717	(the pullback is `Spec` of the tensor product `R[1/x] ⊗_R R[1/y]`) with the
718	localisation identification `R[1/x] ⊗_R R[1/y] ≅ R[1/(xy)]`
719	(`IsLocalization.Away.mul'`, `IsLocalization.algEquiv`). Project-local helper for the
720	triple-overlap pullbacks of the Grassmannian glue data; stated over a general base ring
721	so its proof term carries the needed `IsScalarTower` instances (avoiding a typeclass
722	timeout over the heavy chart ring). -/
723	noncomputable def awayPullbackIso {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
724	    Limits.pullback
725	        (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away x))))
726	        (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y)))) ≅
727	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (x * y))) :=
728	  letI : IsLocalization.Away (x * y)
729	      (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) :=
730	    IsLocalization.Away.mul' (Localization.Away x) _ x y
731	  (pullbackSpecIso A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) ≪≫
732	    Scheme.Spec.mapIso
733	      ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
734	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
735	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).symm.op
736	
737	/-- The first leg of `awayPullbackIso` is the left away-inclusion: under the
738	identification `pullback ≅ Spec R[1/(xy)]`, the projection to `Spec R[1/x]` is
739	`Spec.map` of `awayInclLeft x y`. Project-local: the `pullback.fst`-compatibility
740	needed for the `t_fac` field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
741	theorem awayPullbackIso_inv_fst {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
742	    (awayPullbackIso x y).inv ≫
743	        Limits.pullback.fst
744	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away x))))
745	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y))))
746	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayInclLeft x y)) := by
747	  letI : IsLocalization.Away (x * y)
748	      (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) :=
749	    IsLocalization.Away.mul' (Localization.Away x) _ x y
750	  rw [awayPullbackIso, Iso.trans_inv, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst,
751	    show (Scheme.Spec.mapIso ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
752	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
753	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).symm.op).inv
754	      = Spec.map ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
755	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
756	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).hom from rfl,
757	    ← Spec.map_comp]
758	  congr 1
759	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
760	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, RingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso_hom]
761	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers x)
762	  ext w
763	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, awayInclLeft,
764	    Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom_apply, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq,
765	    ← Algebra.TensorProduct.algebraMap_apply]
766	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
767	    (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
768	    (Localization.Away (x * y))).commutes w
769	
770	/-- The second leg of `awayPullbackIso` is the right away-inclusion: under the
771	identification `pullback ≅ Spec R[1/(xy)]`, the projection to `Spec R[1/y]` is
772	`Spec.map` of `awayInclRight x y`. Project-local: the `pullback.snd`-compatibility
773	needed for the `t_fac` field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
774	theorem awayPullbackIso_inv_snd {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
775	    (awayPullbackIso x y).inv ≫
776	        Limits.pullback.snd
777	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away x))))
778	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y))))
779	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayInclRight x y)) := by
780	  letI : IsLocalization.Away (x * y)
781	      (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) :=
782	    IsLocalization.Away.mul' (Localization.Away x) _ x y
783	  rw [awayPullbackIso, Iso.trans_inv, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd,
784	    show (Scheme.Spec.mapIso ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
785	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
786	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).symm.op).inv
787	      = Spec.map ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
788	        (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
789	        (Localization.Away (x * y))).toRingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso).hom from rfl,
790	    ← Spec.map_comp]
791	  congr 1
792	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
793	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, RingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso_hom]
794	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers y)
795	  ext w
796	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, awayInclRight,
797	    RingHom.coe_coe,
798	    Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_apply, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq]
799	  rw [show (1 : Localization.Away x) ⊗ₜ[A] (algebraMap A (Localization.Away y) w)
800	      = algebraMap A (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y)) w from by
801	        rw [Algebra.TensorProduct.algebraMap_apply, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one,
802	          Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, TensorProduct.tmul_smul, TensorProduct.smul_tmul']]
803	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (x * y))
804	    (TensorProduct A (Localization.Away x) (Localization.Away y))
805	    (Localization.Away (x * y))).commutes w
806	
807	/-- The product-commutativity equivalence `R[1/(xy)] ≃+* R[1/(yx)]`: the two
808	away-localisations agree because `Submonoid.powers (x*y) = Submonoid.powers (y*x)`.
809	Project-local: resolves the product-order mismatch between `cocycleΘIJ` (domain
810	`R[1/(P^J_I · P^J_K)]`) and the target `awayPullbackIso` (codomain `R[1/(P^J_K · P^J_I)]`)
811	in the triple-overlap `t'`-field of the Grassmannian glue data. -/
812	noncomputable def awayMulCommEquiv {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
813	    Localization.Away (x * y) ≃+* Localization.Away (y * x) := by
814	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (y * x) (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
815	    rw [mul_comm y x]; infer_instance
816	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (y * x))
817	    (Localization.Away (x * y)) (Localization.Away (y * x))).toRingEquiv
818	
819	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data
820	
821	We assemble the affine charts, their principal-open overlaps, the transition
822	isomorphisms and the cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by
823	the size-`d` subsets `{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`. Its `.glued` is the
824	Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
825	
826	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
827	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
828	
829	/-- The triple-overlap `t'`-field of the Grassmannian glue data
830	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the morphism
831	`U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K ⟶ U^J_K ×_{U^J} U^J_I` reconciling the two pullbacks via the
832	away-pullback identification, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`, and the
833	order-swap isomorphism. Project-local. -/
834	noncomputable def chartTransition' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
835	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
836	    Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ⟶
837	      Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) :=
838	  (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
839	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
840	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
841	      (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
842	    (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).inv
843	
844	/-- `awayMulCommEquiv` lies over the base ring: it intertwines the two structure maps
845	`R → R[1/(xy)]` and `R → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for the `t_fac`/cocycle
846	ring computations. -/
847	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
848	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (x * y)))
849	      = algebraMap A (Localization.Away (y * x)) := by
850	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (y * x) (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
851	    rw [mul_comm y x]; infer_instance
852	  ext a
853	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (y * x))
854	    (Localization.Away (x * y)) (Localization.Away (y * x))).commutes a
855	
856	/-- The ring-hom identity underlying the `t_fac` coherence field of the Grassmannian
857	glue data: over the triple-overlap rings, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`
858	pre-composed with the order-swap and right inclusion equals the left inclusion
859	post-composed with the plain transition `θ_{I,J}`. Both reduce to
860	`ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
861	theorem chartTransition'_ringIdentity (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
862	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
863	    (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
864	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
865	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
866	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
867	          (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
868	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r J I hJ hI))
869	  -- Reduce both sides to `ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` after precomposing with the structure map of
870	  -- `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
871	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap,
872	    awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
873	    cocycleΘIJ, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
874	    transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
875	
876	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
877	-- The `erw` through the `HasPullback` instance diamond on the heavy `MvPolynomial`
878	-- localisation objects is defeq-expensive; the raised limit covers it.
879	/-- The `t_fac`-compatibility field of the Grassmannian glue data
880	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the triple-overlap transition `t'` is compatible with the
881	projections, `t'_{I,J,K} ≫ pr₂ = pr₁ ≫ t_{I,J}`. Reduces, both pullbacks being
882	affine, to the ring identity `chartTransition'_ringIdentity` via the leg lemmas.
883	Project-local. -/
884	theorem chartTransition'_fac (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
885	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
886	    chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
887	        Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
888	      = Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ≫
889	          chartTransition d r I J hI hJ := by
890	  -- Rewrite the source `pr₁` via the iso (keeping a single `awayPullbackIso` term so the
891	  -- `HasPullback` instance is consistent), then cancel the leg via the iso identity.
892	  have hfstc : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
893	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
894	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
895	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
896	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
897	  have hfst := (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hfstc
898	  -- The pure ring/`Spec` content: the three localised pieces equal the plain transition.
899	  have hXY : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
900	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
901	          (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
902	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
903	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
904	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
905	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) ≫
906	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ)) := by
907	    rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
908	      ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, chartTransition'_ringIdentity]
909	  rw [hfst, chartTransition']
910	  simp only [Category.assoc]
911	  -- `erw` (defeq) to fire the snd-leg lemma through the `HasPullback` instance diamond.
912	  erw [awayPullbackIso_inv_snd]
913	  simp only [chartTransition]
914	  -- `congrArg` + defeq associativity closes it (syntactic `rw`/`Category.assoc` are blocked
915	  -- by the Scheme-category instance diamond on these heavy localisation objects).
916	  exact congrArg (_ ≫ ·) hXY
917	
918	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the rotated triple-overlap ring cocycle `Φ = id`
919	
920	The `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data reduces (after stripping the conjugating
921	pullback isomorphisms) to a single ring identity `Φ = id` over the triple-overlap ring
922	`S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`, where
923	`Φ := Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I` (rotated index triples).
924	We prove it by telescoping with the cocycle condition `cocycleCondition` (collapsing
925	`θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K} = θ_{I,K}`) down to a single inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`,
926	which is closed by the matrix computation `transitionInvImageMatrix` (the `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` collapse
927	of `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq`, run for the inverse pair).
928	
929	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id`
930	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
931	
932	/-- The order-swap absorbs a left away-inclusion into a right one:
933	`swap_{x,y} ∘ ι^L_{x,y} = ι^R_{y,x}` as maps `R[1/x] → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for
934	the rotation lemma `rotMid`. -/
935	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
936	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (awayInclLeft x y) = awayInclRight y x := by
937	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers x)
938	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
939	    awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap]
940	
941	/-- **Rotation lemma** for the triple-overlap transitions: conjugating the rotated
942	`Θ_{J,K,I}` by the two order-swaps recovers the `J,K`-transition `Θ_{J,K}` in the `I,J,K`
943	frame. Both sides are lifts of `θ̃_{J,K}`; checked on the chart ring `R^K`. Project-local. -/
944	private lemma rotMid (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
945	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
946	    ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
947	        (cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI)).comp
948	        (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom
949	      = cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
950	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
951	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ))
952	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, RingHom.comp_assoc, cocycleΘIJ,
953	    IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft,
954	    cocycleΘJK, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
955	
956	/-- The matrix collapse behind the inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`: pushing the image
957	matrix `(X^K_I)⁻¹ X^K` of `θ_{K,I}` forward along `θ_{I,K}` (realised as
958	`cocycleΘIK ∘ ι^L`) recovers the universal matrix `X^I` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I`.

1107	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :

1109	        chartTransition' d r K I J hK hI hJ

1130	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data and scheme
1131	
1132	Assemble the affine charts, principal-open overlaps, transition isomorphisms, and the
1133	cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1134	`{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`; its `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)`
1135	over `ℤ`. Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`. -/
1136	
1137	/-- The **Grassmannian glue data** (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the `Scheme.GlueData` whose
1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued

1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
1187	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
1188	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
1189	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
1190	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1191	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1192	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1193	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1194	  rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]

1196	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map** `δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J`
1197	(`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism of the restricted diagonal
1198	`Δ^{-1}(U^I ×_ℤ U^J) = U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J`. It is the tensor-product lift of the
1199	structure map `R^I → R^I_J` (first factor) and the pre-localisation transition hom
1200	`θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (second factor), so `X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I` and
1201	`1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
1202	noncomputable def diagonalRingMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :=
1204	  Algebra.TensorProduct.lift
1205	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1206	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
1207	    (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
1208	    (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)

1210	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the left factor is the structure map: `δ_{I,J}(a ⊗ 1) = a` in `R^I_J`.

1212	theorem diagonalRingMap_left (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1213	    (hJ : J.card = d) (a : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :
1214	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] 1)
1215	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a := by
1216	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, mul_one]
1217	  rfl

1219	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the right factor is the pre-localisation transition hom:
1220	`δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ b) = θ̃_{I,J}(b)`. Project-local. -/
1221	theorem diagonalRingMap_right (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1222	    (hJ : J.card = d) (b : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ) :
1223	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (1 ⊗ₜ[ℤ] b) = transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ b := by

1226	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map is surjective** (`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism
1227	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J` of the restricted diagonal is surjective, so the
1228	restricted diagonal `U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J` is a closed immersion. The image contains the
1229	structure-map image of `R^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (right
1230	factor), which together generate `R^I_J = R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local. -/
1231	theorem diagonalRingMap_surjective (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1232	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Function.Surjective (diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
1233	  intro z
1234	  -- `z · uⁿ = algebraMap a` for some `a` and `n = ` power of `P^I_J`, by the localisation
1235	  -- property, where `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1236	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) z
1237	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1238	  -- The witness pushes the power into the second tensor factor: `a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ`, avoiding
1239	  -- any arithmetic in the tensor-product ring.
1240	  refine ⟨a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (minorDet d r J I hJ hI ^ n), ?_⟩
1241	  -- `v := θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is the inverse of `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1242	  have hvu : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1243	      algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1244	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 :=
1245	    transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul d r I J hI hJ
1246	  -- `algebraMap ↑s = uⁿ`.
1247	  have hsu : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1248	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (s : _)
1249	      = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1250	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1251	    rw [← hn, map_pow]
1252	  -- `algebraMap a = z · uⁿ`.
1253	  have key : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1254	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a
1255	      = z * (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1256	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1257	    rw [← hs, hsu]
1258	  -- `δ(a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ) = algebraMap a · vⁿ = z · uⁿ · vⁿ = z · (u·v)ⁿ = z`.
1259	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_pow,
1260	    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', key, mul_assoc, ← mul_pow, mul_comm _
1261	      (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)), hvu, one_pow, mul_one]
1262	
1263	/-- The intersection pullback `U^I ∩ U^J = pullback (ι i) (ι j)` is the chart overlap
1264	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`lem:gr_separated`, source identification): from the glue-data
1265	universal property `theGlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit`, whose cone point is `V (i,j) = chartOverlap`.
1266	This is the `e₂` source iso of the restricted-diagonal closed-immersion argument. Project-local. -/
1267	noncomputable def pullbackιIso (d r : ℕ) (i j : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1268	    Limits.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι i) ((theGlueData d r).ι j) ≅
1269	      chartOverlap d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 :=
1270	  (Limits.limit.isLimit _).conePointUniqueUpToIso
1271	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j)
1272	
1273	/-! ### Separatedness of the Grassmannian scheme
1274	
1275	The blueprint target `lem:gr_separated` (`Grassmannian.isSeparated : (scheme d r).IsSeparated`)
1276	reduces, via `Scheme.isSeparated_iff` and `IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.of_openCover` applied to the
1277	patch cover `Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight (theGlueData d r).openCover (theGlueData d r).openCover`
1278	of `scheme ×_⊤ scheme`, to showing that on each patch `U^I ×_⊤ U^J` the restricted diagonal is a
1279	closed immersion. Exactly as in `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.isSeparated`
1280	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean`), `pullbackDiagonalMapIdIso` rewrites
1281	this to the affine morphism `Spec.map δ_{I,J}`, a closed immersion by
1282	`IsClosedImmersion.spec_of_surjective` and `diagonalRingMap_surjective` (proved above — the only
1283	mathematically substantive ingredient). The remaining geometric assembly (source iso
1284	`pullback (ι i) (ι j) ≅ chartOverlap` from `theGlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit`; the target iso
1285	`pullback (ι i ≫ ⊤) (ι j ≫ ⊤) ≅ Spec(R^I ⊗_ℤ R^J)` via `pullbackSpecIso ℤ R^I R^J`, which
1286	requires reconciling the categorical terminal `⊤_Scheme` with `Spec ℤ`; and the two-legs
1287	`pullback.hom_ext` coherence matching `δ_{I,J} ∘ includeLeft = algebraMap`,
1288	`δ_{I,J} ∘ includeRight = transitionMap ∘ incl = transitionPreMap`) is the remaining work for the
1289	next iteration. -/

1	\chapter{The Grassmannian over $\mathbb{Z}$: affine charts and gluing}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianCells}
3	
4	\section{Set-up: charts indexed by $d$-subsets}
5	\label{sec:gr_setup}
6	
7	Fix integers \(r \ge d \ge 1\). For a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M\) over a ring
8	and a subset \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\), write \(M_I\)
9	for the \emph{\(I\)-th minor} of \(M\): the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose
10	columns are those indexed by \(I\). We construct the Grassmannian scheme
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing one affine chart \(U^I\) for
12	each such \(I\). The construction is, conceptually, the quotient
13	\(\mathrm{GL}_{d,\mathbb{Z}} \backslash V\) of the scheme \(V\) of rank-\(d\)
14	matrices, but we never use the language of group-scheme actions: the charts and
15	their transition maps are elementary matrix algebra over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Taking
16	\(d = 1\) recovers \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,1) = \mathbb{P}^{r-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\).
17	
18	\begin{definition}
19	
20	\leanok
21	[Affine chart \(U^I\)]
22	  \label{def:gr_affine_chart}
23	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart}
24	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
25	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
26	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
27	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
28	  % "If $M$ is a $d\times r$-matrix, and
29	  %  $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with cardinality $\#(I)$ equal
30	  %  to $d$, the $I$ th minor $M_I$ of $M$ will
31	  %  mean the $d\times d$ minor of $M$ whose columns are indexed by
32	  %  $I$.
33	  %
34	  %  For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
35	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
36	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
37	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$.
38	  %  Let $\Z[X^I]$ denote the polynomial ring in the variables
39	  %  $x^I_{p,q}$, and let $U^I = \spec \Z[X^I]$, which is non-canonically
40	  %  isomorphic to the affine space $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$."
41	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
42	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). Let \(X^I\) denote the
43	  \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\)
44	  identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\) and whose remaining \(d(r - d)\) entries are
45	  independent indeterminates \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\),
46	  \(q \in \{1,\dots,r\} \setminus I\)) over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Write
47	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) for the polynomial ring in these \(d(r-d)\) variables and
48	  set
49	  \[
50	    U^I \;:=\; \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
51	  \]
52	  The choice of an ordering of the free entries gives a (non-canonical)
53	  isomorphism \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\); in particular
54	  \(U^I\) is a smooth affine \(\mathbb{Z}\)-scheme of relative dimension
55	  \(d(r-d)\). Intuitively a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-point of \(U^I\) is a \(d \times r\)
56	  matrix whose \(I\)-columns form the identity, i.e.\ a rank-\(d\) quotient of
57	  \(\mathbb{Z}^{r}\) trivialised on the coordinates \(I\).
58	\end{definition}
59	
60	\section{Transition maps and the cocycle condition}
61	\label{sec:gr_transition}
62	
63	\subsection*{Mathlib inputs for the transition map}
64	
65	The construction of \(\theta_{I,J}\) is a Cramer-inverse computation over the
66	localised chart ring, followed by one application of the universal property of a
67	localisation. We record the four Mathlib facts it rests on as dependency
68	anchors.
69	
70	\begin{lemma}
71	[Localised element is a unit]
72	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
73	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit}
74	  \mathlibok
75	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
76	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S\) a localisation of \(R\)
77	  away from \(x\) (i.e.\ \(S = R[1/x]\)). Then the image of \(x\) under the
78	  structure map \(R \to S\) is a unit of \(S\).
79	\end{lemma}
80	
81	\begin{lemma}
82	[Left Cramer inverse]
83	  \label{lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul}
84	  \lean{Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul}
85	  \mathlibok
86	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
87	  For a square matrix \(A\) over a commutative ring with \(\det A\) a unit, the
88	  nonsingular inverse \(A^{-1}\) satisfies \(A^{-1} A = 1\).
89	\end{lemma}
90	
91	\begin{lemma}
92	[Right Cramer inverse]
93	  \label{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
94	  \lean{Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv}
95	  \mathlibok
96	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
97	  For a square matrix \(A\) over a commutative ring with \(\det A\) a unit, the
98	  nonsingular inverse \(A^{-1}\) satisfies \(A A^{-1} = 1\).
99	\end{lemma}
100	
101	\begin{lemma}
102	[Universal property of an away-localisation]
103	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_lift}
104	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.lift}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
107	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S = R[1/x]\) a localisation
108	  of \(R\) away from \(x\). Given any ring homomorphism \(g : R \to P\) that
109	  sends \(x\) to a unit of \(P\), there is a unique ring homomorphism
110	  \(S \to P\) extending \(g\) along \(R \to S\).
111	\end{lemma}
112	
113	\subsection*{The transition map by matrix algebra}
114	
115	We now build \(\theta_{I,J}\) in seven steps over the localised chart ring
116	\(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), splitting at the matrix-algebra seams. The
117	ambient chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) is the polynomial ring of
118	\cref{def:gr_affine_chart} in the \(d(r-d)\) free variables \(x^I_{p,q}\)
119	(\(p \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), \(q \notin I\)).
120	
121	\begin{definition}
122	
123	\leanok
124	[The universal matrix \(X^I\)]
125	  \label{def:gr_universal_matrix}
126	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix}
127	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart}
128	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
129	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
130	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
131	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
132	  % "For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
133	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
134	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
135	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$."
136	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). With the chart ring
137	  %   `R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, define
138	  %   `universalMatrix d r I : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R^I` whose entry at
139	  %   `(p, q)` is: the identity entry `if (orderIso I) p = q then 1 else 0`
140	  %   when `q ∈ I` (using the order isomorphism `Fin d ≃o I` from `#I = d`),
141	  %   and the indeterminate `MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩)` when `q ∉ I`.
142	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
143	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). The \emph{universal
144	  matrix} \(X^I\) is the \(d \times r\) matrix over the chart ring
145	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) whose \(I\)-minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\) identity
146	  \(1_{d\times d}\) and whose remaining entries are the free indeterminates
147	  \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\), \(q \notin I\)). It is the universal point of
148	  the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}): a ring map
149	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to A\) is the same as a \(d \times r\) matrix over \(A\)
150	  with identity \(I\)-minor.
151	\end{definition}
152	
153	\begin{definition}
154	
155	\leanok
156	[The minor determinant \(P^I_J\)]
157	  \label{def:gr_minor_det}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.minorDet}
159	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix}
160	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
161	  % affine patches" (read from
162	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
163	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
164	  % "For any $J\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(J) =d$, let
165	  %  $P^I_J = \det(X^I_J) \in \Z[X^I]$
166	  %  where $X^I_J$ is the $J$ th minor of $X^I$."
167	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). `minorDet d r I J : R^I` is the
168	  %   determinant of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose
169	  %   columns are indexed by `J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order
170	  %   isomorphism `Fin d ≃o J` coming from `#J = d`:
171	  %   `(universalMatrix d r I).submatrix id (orderIso J) |>.det`.
172	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
173	  For \(J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(J) = d\), let \(X^I_J\) denote the
174	  \(J\)-th minor of the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix})
175	  -- the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose columns are those indexed by \(J\). Set
176	  \[
177	    P^I_J \;:=\; \det\bigl(X^I_J\bigr) \;\in\; \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
178	  \]
179	  Then \(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) (the localisation of the chart ring
180	  away from \(P^I_J\)) is the ring of the principal open subscheme
181	  \(U^I_J = \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J] \subseteq U^I\) on which \(P^I_J\) is
182	  invertible. One has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\), so \(R^I_I =
183	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) and \(U^I_I = U^I\).
184	\end{definition}

268	\begin{lemma}

302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix}
303	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_universal_matrix}
304	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
305	  % affine patches" (read from
306	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
307	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
308	  % "The images of the variables
309	  %  $x^J_{p,q}$ are given by the entries of the matrix formula
310	  %  $\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J) ^{-1}\,X^I$."
311	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
312	  %   `imageMatrix d r I J : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J))`
313	  %   `:= universalMinorInv d r I J * (universalMatrix d r I).map (algebraMap _ _)`.
314	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
315	  Define the \(d \times r\) matrix over \(R^I_J\)
316	  \[
317	    M \;:=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I \;\in\; \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(R^I_J),
318	  \]
319	  the product of the Cramer inverse (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}) with the
320	  universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) base-changed to
321	  \(R^I_J\). Its entries are the prospective images of the indeterminates
322	  \(x^J_{p,q}\) under \(\theta_{I,J}\). Its \(J\)-minor is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_J =
323	  1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so \(M\) is a
324	  point of the chart \(U^J\); and its \(I\)-minor is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I =
325	  (X^I_J)^{-1}\).
326	\end{definition}
327	
328	\begin{definition}
329	
330	\leanok
331	[The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\)]
332	  \label{def:gr_transition_pre}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap}
334	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix}
335	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
336	  % affine patches" (read from
337	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
338	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
339	  % "a ring homomorphism
340	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
341	  %  defined as follows. The images of the variables
342	  %  $x^J_{p,q}$ are given by the entries of the matrix formula
343	  %  $\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J) ^{-1}\,X^I$."
344	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). Via the universal property of the
345	  %   polynomial chart ring `R^J := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ`,
346	  %   `transitionPreMap d r I J : R^J →ₐ[ℤ] Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`
347	  %   `:= MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J e.1 e.2.1)`, i.e. the
348	  %   free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}` is sent to the `(p,q)`-entry of `imageMatrix`.
349	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
350	  Let \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\) be the
351	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism out of the chart ring of \(J\) determined,
352	  via the universal property of the polynomial ring, by sending each free
353	  indeterminate \(x^J_{p,q}\) to the \((p,q)\)-entry of the image matrix \(M =
354	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}); equivalently
355	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) as a matrix identity (the
356	  identity \(J\)-block of \(X^J\) matching the identity \(J\)-minor of \(M\)).
357	\end{definition}
358	
359	\begin{lemma}
360	
361	\leanok
362	[The pre-hom sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit]
363	  \label{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}
364	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
365	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
366	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
367	  % affine patches" (read from
368	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
370	  % "In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
371	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
372	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
373	  %   `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J :`
374	  %   `IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J (minorDet d r J I))`.
375	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
376	  The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre})
377	  sends \(P^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}, the \(I\)-minor determinant of
378	  \(X^J\)) to a unit of \(R^I_J\). Indeed
379	  \[
380	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr)
381	      = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1} = 1/P^I_J,
382	  \]
383	  which is a unit.
384	\end{lemma}
385	\begin{proof}
386	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
387	  \leanok
388	  By \cref{def:gr_transition_pre}, \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = M = (X^I_J)^{-1}
389	  X^I\), so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries the \(I\)-minor \(X^J_I\) of \(X^J\) to
390	  the \(I\)-minor of \(M\), which is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) since
391	  the \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity. Hence \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) =
392	  \det((X^I_J)^{-1}) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\). As \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit
393	  (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), so is its inverse; the matrix-inverse
394	  determinant identity uses \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}.
395	\end{proof}
396	
397	\begin{definition}
398	
399	\leanok
400	[Transition map \(\theta_{I,J}\)]
401	  \label{def:gr_transition}
402	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap}
403	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:mathlib_away_lift, def:gr_affine_chart}
404	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
405	  % affine patches" (read from
406	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
407	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
408	  % "For any $I$ and $J$, a ring homomorphism
409	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
410	  %  defined as follows. ...
411	  %  In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
412	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
413	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). The away-localisation lift of the
414	  %   pre-hom along `P^J_I`:
415	  %   `transitionMap d r I J :`
416	  %   `Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`
417	  %   `:= IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I)`
418	  %   `(isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J)` applied to
419	  %   `(transitionPreMap d r I J : R^J →+* R^I_J)`.
420	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
421	  Because the pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to
422	  R^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit of \(R^I_J\)
423	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}), the universal property of the
424	  away-localisation (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) provides a unique ring
425	  homomorphism extending it along \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I]\).
426	  This extension is the \emph{transition map}
427	  \[
428	    \theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \longrightarrow
429	    \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J], \qquad \theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I .
430	  \]
431	  Dually \(\theta_{I,J}\) is the comorphism of a morphism of schemes
432	  \(U^I_J \to U^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), which we also denote
433	  \(\theta_{I,J}\); it sends the universal matrix \(X^I\) to \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\).
434	\end{definition}
435	
436	\begin{lemma}
437	
438	\leanok
439	[\(\theta_{I,I}\) is the identity]
440	  \label{lem:gr_transition_self}
441	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap_self}
442	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
443	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
444	  % affine patches" (read from
445	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L838-L848).
446	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
447	  % "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$".
448	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
449	  %   `transitionMap_self d r I : transitionMap d r I I = RingHom.id _`
450	  %   (up to the identification `R^I_I = R^I` from `P^I_I = 1`).
451	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
452	  For every \(I\), \(\theta_{I,I} = \id_{U^I}\): since \(P^I_I = 1\)
453	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) the minor \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\) is its own
454	  inverse, so \(\theta_{I,I}(X^I) = (X^I_I)^{-1} X^I = X^I\), i.e.\
455	  \(\theta_{I,I}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity ring homomorphism
456	  of \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_I] = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\).
457	\end{lemma}
458	\begin{proof}
459	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
460	  \leanok
461	  When \(J = I\) one has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\)
462	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}), so \(R^I_I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\), the minor \(X^I_I\)
463	  is the identity, and its Cramer inverse is again the identity. Hence the image
464	  matrix is \(M = 1 \cdot X^I = X^I\), the pre-hom sends each \(x^I_{p,q}\) to
465	  itself, and its localisation lift (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity.
466	\end{proof}

468	\subsection*{Project-local matrix and minor bookkeeping}
469	
470	The cocycle computation rests on a handful of elementary matrix-algebra
471	identities over the (localised) chart rings. They are project-internal helpers
472	with no external source; each is verified directly in Lean.
473	
474	\begin{lemma}
475	
476	\leanok
477	[Column submatrix of a product]
478	  \label{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}
479	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.mul_submatrix_col}
480	  Let \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(R)\) and
481	  \(B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(R)\) be matrices over a commutative ring,
482	  and \(g : \{1,\dots,d\} \to \{1,\dots,r\}\) a column-index map. Then taking the
483	  column submatrix of the product equals multiplying by the column submatrix of
484	  the right factor:
485	  \[
486	    (A B)_{\bullet\, g} \;=\; A \,\bigl(B_{\bullet\, g}\bigr).
487	  \]
488	\end{lemma}
489	\begin{proof}
490	
491	\leanok
492	  Entrywise from the definition of matrix multiplication (reindexing the rows by
493	  the identity leaves the contraction index untouched). Proved directly in Lean.
494	\end{proof}
495	
496	\begin{lemma}
497	
498	\leanok
499	[A ring map commutes with the Cramer inverse]
500	  \label{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
501	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.map_nonsing_inv}
502	  \uses{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
503	  Let \(f : R \to S\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and
504	  \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{n\times n}(R)\) a square matrix with \(\det A\) a
505	  unit. Writing \(f_\ast\) for the entrywise application of \(f\), the
506	  nonsingular inverse is natural:
507	  \[
508	    \bigl(f_\ast A\bigr)^{-1} \;=\; f_\ast\bigl(A^{-1}\bigr).
509	  \]
510	\end{lemma}
511	\begin{proof}
512	
513	\leanok
514	  Since \(f_\ast A \cdot f_\ast(A^{-1}) = f_\ast(A A^{-1}) = f_\ast(1_{n\times n})
515	  = 1_{n\times n}\) (using \(A A^{-1} = 1\), \cref{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}),
516	  the matrix \(f_\ast(A^{-1})\) is a right inverse of \(f_\ast A\), hence equals
517	  \((f_\ast A)^{-1}\). Proved directly in Lean.
518	\end{proof}
519	
520	\begin{lemma}
521	
522	\leanok
523	[Functoriality of entrywise application]
524	  \label{lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp}
525	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.map_map_eq_of_comp}
526	  Let \(f : R \to A\), \(g : A \to D\) and \(h : R \to D\) be ring
527	  homomorphisms with \(g \circ f = h\). Then for any matrix \(M\) over \(R\),
528	  applying \(f\) then \(g\) entrywise equals applying \(h\) entrywise:
529	  \(g_\ast(f_\ast M) = h_\ast M\).
530	\end{lemma}
531	\begin{proof}
532	
533	\leanok
534	  Immediate from functoriality of \(\operatorname{Mat}(-)\) (composition of
535	  entrywise maps). Proved directly in Lean.
536	\end{proof}
537	
538	\begin{lemma}
539	
540	\leanok
541	[Inverse cancellation in a composite]
542	  \label{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}
543	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}
544	  \uses{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
545	  Let \(A, B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(R)\) and
546	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times e}(R)\) over a commutative ring, with
547	  \(\det A\) a unit. Then
548	  \[
549	    \bigl(B^{-1} A\bigr)\,\bigl(A^{-1} M\bigr) \;=\; B^{-1} M.
550	  \]
551	\end{lemma}
552	\begin{proof}
553	
554	\leanok
555	  By associativity and \(A A^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\)
556	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}). Proved directly in Lean.
557	\end{proof}
558	
559	\begin{lemma}
560	
561	\leanok
562	[The \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity]
563	  \label{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
564	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
565	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix}
566	  The \(I\)-minor of the universal matrix \(X^I\)
567	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), with its \(I\)-columns read through the order
568	  isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \xrightarrow{\sim} I\), is the \(d\times d\)
569	  identity: \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\). (This is the normalisation \(P^I_I = 1\)
570	  defining the chart.)
571	\end{lemma}
572	\begin{proof}
573	
574	\leanok
575	  By \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix} the \(q\)-column with \(q \in I\) is the
576	  standard basis vector \(e_p\) where \(p\) is the preimage of \(q\) under the
577	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \xrightarrow{\sim} I\); so the \(I\)-minor
578	  is the identity. Proved directly in Lean.
579	\end{proof}
580	
581	\begin{lemma}
582	
583	\leanok
584	[The \(J\)-minor of the image matrix is the identity]
585	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
586	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
587	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}
588	  The \(J\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
589	  (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}) is the identity:
590	  \[
591	    M_J \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_J \;=\; 1_{d\times d}.
592	  \]
593	\end{lemma}
594	\begin{proof}
595	
596	\leanok
597	  The \(J\)-column submatrix of \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) is
598	  \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) times the \(J\)-column submatrix of \(X^I\), i.e.
599	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_J\), by \cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}; this is
600	  \(1_{d\times d}\) by the left-inverse identity
601	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}). Proved directly in Lean.
602	\end{proof}
603	
604	\begin{lemma}
605	
606	\leanok
607	[The \(I\)-minor of the image matrix is the Cramer inverse]
608	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_I}
609	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_submatrix_I}
610	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
611	  The \(I\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
612	  (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}) is the Cramer inverse:
613	  \[
614	    M_I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}.
615	  \]
616	\end{lemma}
617	\begin{proof}
618	
619	\leanok
620	  By \cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col} the \(I\)-column submatrix of \(M\) is
621	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\), and \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
622	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}). Proved directly in Lean.
623	\end{proof}
624	
625	\begin{lemma}
626	
627	\leanok
628	[\(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) realises the matrix formula]
629	  \label{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
630	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
631	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_image_matrix, def:gr_transition_pre, lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
632	  Applying the pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)
633	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) entrywise to the universal matrix \(X^J\) yields
634	  the image matrix:
635	  \[
636	    (\tilde\theta_{I,J})_\ast\, X^J \;=\; M \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I .
637	  \]
638	\end{lemma}
639	\begin{proof}
640	
641	\leanok
642	  On a free entry \(x^J_{p,q}\) (\(q \notin J\)) this holds by the definition of
643	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) as evaluation at the entries of \(M\)
644	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}). On a column \(q \in J\) the entry of \(X^J\) is
645	  an identity-block entry, which \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries to the
646	  corresponding entry of \(M_J = 1_{d\times d}\)
647	  (\cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}). Proved directly in Lean.
648	\end{proof}
649	
650	\begin{lemma}
651	
652	\leanok
653	[Image of a cross minor under \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)]
654	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
655	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet}
656	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
657	  For a third size-\(d\) subset \(K\), the pre-hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) sends the
658	  minor determinant \(P^J_K\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) to the determinant of the
659	  \(K\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\):
660	  \[
661	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_K\bigr) \;=\; \det\bigl(M_K\bigr).
662	  \]
663	\end{lemma}
664	\begin{proof}
665	
666	\leanok
667	  The hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) commutes with determinants and with passing to
668	  a column submatrix, so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_K) =
669	  \det\bigl((\tilde\theta_{I,J})_\ast X^J\bigr)_K\); apply
670	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}. Proved directly in Lean.
671	\end{proof}
672	
673	\begin{lemma}
674	
675	\leanok
676	[Naturality of the image matrix]
677	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}
678	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_map_eq}
679	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv, lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_universal_minor}
680	  Let \(X\) be a size-\(d\) subset and \(\iota : R^I_X \to D\) a ring
681	  homomorphism lying over the structure map \(R^I \to D\), i.e.\
682	  \(\iota \circ (R^I \to R^I_X) = (R^I \to D)\). Writing \(Y\) for the universal
683	  matrix \(X^I\) base-changed to \(D\) and \(Y_X\) for its \(X\)-minor, one has
684	  \[
685	    \iota_\ast\bigl((X^I_X)^{-1} X^I\bigr) \;=\; (Y_X)^{-1}\, Y .
686	  \]
687	\end{lemma}
688	\begin{proof}
689	
690	\leanok
691	  Distribute \(\iota_\ast\) across the product \(M = (X^I_X)^{-1} X^I\); the
692	  factor \((X^I_X)^{-1}\) passes through \(\iota_\ast\) as a Cramer inverse
693	  (\cref{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}, using \cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), and both
694	  factors collapse to base changes over \(D\) by functoriality
695	  (\cref{lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp}) since \(\iota\) lies over \(R^I \to D\).
696	  Proved directly in Lean.
697	\end{proof}
698	
699	\subsection*{Triple-overlap rings and the localised transition maps}
700	
701	The cocycle condition \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\) cannot
702	be stated as a naive composition of the landed transition maps of
703	\cref{def:gr_transition}: the codomain of \(\theta_{J,K}\) (the ring
704	\(R^J[1/P^J_K]\)) differs from the domain of \(\theta_{I,J}\) (the ring
705	\(R^J[1/P^J_I]\)). The identity therefore lives over the \emph{triple-overlap}
706	rings obtained by inverting both relevant minors in each chart ring:
707	\[
708	  S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)], \quad
709	  S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)], \quad
710	  S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)].
711	\]
712	
713	\begin{definition}
714	
715	\leanok
716	[Inclusion of an away-localisation into a double one (left)]
717	  \label{def:gr_away_incl_left}
718	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclLeft}
719	  \uses{lem:mathlib_away_lift, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
720	  For \(x, y \in R\), the canonical inclusion
721	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y} : R[1/x] \to R[1/(xy)]\) inverting the extra factor
722	  \(y\), defined as the away-localisation lift (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) of
723	  the structure map \(R \to R[1/(xy)]\) along \(x\) — well-defined because \(x\)
724	  is a unit of \(R[1/(xy)]\) (a factor of the unit \(xy\),
725	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}).
726	\end{definition}
727	
728	\begin{definition}
729	
730	\leanok
731	[Inclusion of an away-localisation into a double one (right)]
732	  \label{def:gr_away_incl_right}
733	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclRight}
734	  \uses{lem:mathlib_away_lift, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
735	  For \(x, y \in R\), the canonical inclusion
736	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y} : R[1/y] \to R[1/(xy)]\) inverting the extra factor
737	  \(x\), defined as the away-localisation lift (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) of
738	  \(R \to R[1/(xy)]\) along \(y\) — well-defined since \(y\) is a unit of
739	  \(R[1/(xy)]\) (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}).
740	\end{definition}
741	
742	\begin{lemma}
743	
744	\leanok
745	[Left inclusion lies over \(R\)]
746	  \label{lem:gr_awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap}
747	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap}
748	  \uses{def:gr_away_incl_left}
749	  The inclusion \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left})
750	  intertwines the two structure maps:
751	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y} \circ (R \to R[1/x]) = (R \to R[1/(xy)])\).
752	\end{lemma}
753	\begin{proof}
754	
755	\leanok
756	  By the defining property of the away-localisation lift
757	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}). Proved directly in Lean.
758	\end{proof}
759	
760	\begin{lemma}
761	
762	\leanok
763	[Right inclusion lies over \(R\)]
764	  \label{lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap}
765	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap}
766	  \uses{def:gr_away_incl_right}
767	  The inclusion \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right})
768	  intertwines the two structure maps:
769	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y} \circ (R \to R[1/y]) = (R \to R[1/(xy)])\).
770	\end{lemma}
771	\begin{proof}
772	
773	\leanok
774	  By the defining property of the away-localisation lift
775	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}). Proved directly in Lean.
776	\end{proof}

778	\begin{lemma}
779	
780	\leanok
781	[Left factor is a unit in the double localisation]
782	  \label{lem:gr_isUnit_algebraMap_away_left}
783	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left}
784	  \uses{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}

790	  \(xy\) is a unit of \(R[1/(xy)]\)

794	
795	\begin{lemma}

799	  \label{lem:gr_isUnit_algebraMap_away_right}

803	\end{lemma}
804	\begin{proof}

808	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}); a factor of a unit is a unit.
809	  Proved directly in Lean.

816	  \label{lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross}

818	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, def:gr_image_matrix}

1712	\label{sec:gr_separated}
1713	
1714	The surjectivity of the restricted-diagonal comorphism is the ring-theoretic
1715	heart of separatedness. We isolate it through the following sequence of
1716	helper results before stating the keystone lemma.
1717	
1718	\begin{lemma}
1719	[Two-sided inverse identity \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\,P^I_J = 1\)]
1720	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1721	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1722	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1723	  In \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), the pre-localisation transition hom
1724	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) satisfies the explicit
1725	  two-sided inverse identity
1726	  \[
1727	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_I\bigr) \cdot P^I_J \;=\; 1 .
1728	  \]
1729	  This refines the unit assertion of \cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit} to a named
1730	  inverse: \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the explicit inverse of \(P^I_J\) in
1731	  \(R^I_J\).
1732	\end{lemma}
1733	\begin{proof}
1734	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1735	  By \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet} (taking the third subset \(K = I\)),
1736	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the determinant of the \(I\)-minor of the image
1737	  matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), which by
1738	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap} equals
1739	  \(\det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\). Hence
1740	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\), and multiplying by \(P^I_J =
1741	  \det(X^I_J)\) gives \(\det(X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot \det(X^I_J) = 1\) by the
1742	  localisation-inverse cancellation for the unit \(\det(X^I_J)\)
1743	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}).
1744	\end{proof}
1745	
1746	\begin{definition}
1747	[Restricted-diagonal comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1748	  \label{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1749	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap}
1750	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre}
1751	  The \emph{restricted-diagonal comorphism} on the patch \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
1752	  U^J\) is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism
1753	  \[
1754	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
1755	    \longrightarrow R^I_J,
1756	    \qquad
1757	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad
1758	    1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
1759	  \]
1760	  whose surjectivity is the content of separatedness; this is the \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1761	  of \cref{lem:gr_separated}.
1762	\end{definition}
1763	\begin{proof}
1764	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre}
1765	  Construct \(\delta_{I,J}\) as the tensor-product lift of the two commuting
1766	  factors: the structure map \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] = R^I \to R^I_J\) on the left
1767	  factor, and the pre-localisation transition hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)
1768	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) on the right factor. Since both land in the
1769	  commutative ring \(R^I_J\), the universal property of the tensor product of
1770	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebras yields a unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism out
1771	  of \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\) restricting to these
1772	  factors; by construction its right component is \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\).
1773	\end{proof}
1774	
1775	\begin{lemma}
1776	[Left component of \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1777	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left}
1778	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_left}
1779	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1780	  For \(a \in \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\), the comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1781	  (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) acts on the left factor by the structure map:
1782	  \[
1783	    \delta_{I,J}(a \otimes 1) \;=\; \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a),
1784	  \]
1785	  the image of \(a\) under \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] = R^I \to R^I_J\).
1786	\end{lemma}
1787	\begin{proof}
1788	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1789	  Immediate from the defining tensor-product lift of
1790	  \cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}: the value on a left elementary tensor \(a \otimes
1791	  1\) is the left factor evaluated at \(a\), i.e.\ the structure map.
1792	\end{proof}
1793	
1794	\begin{lemma}
1795	[Right component of \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1796	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right}
1797	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_right}
1798	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1799	  For \(b \in \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\), the comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1800	  (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) acts on the right factor by the pre-localisation
1801	  transition hom:
1802	  \[
1803	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes b) \;=\; \tilde\theta_{I,J}(b)
1804	  \]
1805	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}).
1806	\end{lemma}
1807	\begin{proof}
1808	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1809	  Immediate from the defining tensor-product lift of
1810	  \cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}: the value on a right elementary tensor \(1 \otimes
1811	  b\) is the right factor \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) evaluated at \(b\).
1812	\end{proof}
1813	
1814	\begin{lemma}
1815	[The restricted-diagonal comorphism is surjective]
1816	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1817	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1818	  \uses{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1819	  The comorphism \(\delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}
1820	  \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) is surjective.
1821	\end{lemma}
1822	\begin{proof}
1823	  \uses{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1824	  Let \(z \in R^I_J\). Since \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) is the
1825	  localisation of \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) away from \(P^I_J\), surjectivity of
1826	  the localisation map provides \(a \in R^I\) and \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) with
1827	  \[
1828	    z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \;=\; \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a).
1829	  \]
1830	  Consider the witness \(a \otimes (P^J_I)^n \in \mathbb{Z}[X^I]
1831	  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\). Using multiplicativity together with
1832	  \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left} and \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right},
1833	  \[
1834	    \delta_{I,J}\bigl(a \otimes (P^J_I)^n\bigr)
1835	      = \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a) \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1836	      = z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1837	      = z \cdot \bigl(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\bigr)^n
1838	      = z,
1839	  \]
1840	  where the last equality uses \(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1\) from
1841	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}. Hence \(z\) lies in the image
1842	  of \(\delta_{I,J}\), so \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective.
1843	\end{proof}
1844	
1845	\begin{definition}
1846	[Source pullback iso \(e_2\) for the diagonal computation]
1847	  \label{def:gr_pullbackιIso}
1848	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.pullbackιIso}
1849	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chart_overlap}
1850	  The \emph{source pullback iso} \(e_2\) identifies the categorical fibre product
1851	  of two chart inclusions of the glued scheme (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) with
1852	  the overlap chart:
1853	  \[
1854	    e_2 : \mathrm{pullback}\,(\iota_i)\,(\iota_j) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1855	    U^I \cap U^J \;=\; \texttt{chartOverlap}\,(d,r,i,j)
1856	  \]
1857	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}), where \(\iota_i, \iota_j\) are the open
1858	  immersions of the charts \(U^I, U^J\) into \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
1859	\end{definition}
1860	\begin{proof}
1861	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chart_overlap}
1862	  The glue data of \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} supplies, for each pair \((i,j)\), a
1863	  pullback cone over \(\iota_i, \iota_j\) with apex the overlap chart \(U^I \cap
1864	  U^J\), and this cone is a limit cone. Comparing it with the categorical
1865	  pullback limit cone via the uniqueness-up-to-isomorphism of limit cone points
1866	  yields the desired isomorphism \(e_2\).
1867	\end{proof}
1868	
1869	\begin{lemma}
1870	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
1871	  \label{lem:gr_separated}
1872	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparated}
1873	  % NOTE (iter-033): the pinned decl `Grassmannian.isSeparated` does NOT yet exist.
1874	  % The ring-theoretic heart IS landed axiom-clean (diagonalRingMap + diagonalRingMap_surjective:
1875	  % the restricted-diagonal comorphism is surjective) along with the e₂ source iso pullbackιIso.
1876	  % Remaining: the multi-piece glue assembly (per-patch closed immersion from the surjective
1877	  % comorphism, target iso, terminal-vs-Spec ℤ reconciliation, two-leg hom_ext). A sorry'd
1878	  % isSeparated_dev stub was built then removed under the no-sorry invariant; only a doc-comment
1879	  % reduction remains in the Lean file. See task_results/.../GrassmannianCells.lean.md route (b).
1880	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1881	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Separatedness" (read from
1882	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860).
1883	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1884	  % "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with $U^I\times U^J$
1885	  %  can be seen to be the closed subscheme $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$
1886	  %  defined by entries of the matrix formula $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$,
1887	  %  and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
1888	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1889	  The structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
1890	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is separated; equivalently, the diagonal
1891	  \(\Delta : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
1892	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a closed immersion.
1893	\end{lemma}
1894	
1895	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with
1896	% $U^I\times U^J$ can be seen to be the closed subscheme
1897	% $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
1898	% $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$, and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
1899	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860.)
1900	\begin{proof}
1901	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1902	  The products \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), as \(I, J\) range over the
1903	  size-\(d\) subsets, form an affine open cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
1904	  \times_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Separatedness may be checked on this
1905	  cover: it suffices to show that for each pair \((I,J)\) the preimage
1906	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J)\) maps by a closed immersion into
1907	  \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), i.e.\ that the corresponding ring
1908	  homomorphism is surjective.
1909	
1910	  By \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} the diagonal preimage is the overlap
1911	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J) = U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J = \Spec
1912	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\). The restricted diagonal corresponds to the ring map
1913	  \[
1914	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
1915	    \longrightarrow \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J],
1916	    \qquad
1917	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad 1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
1918	  \]
1919	  the second component being the comorphism \(\theta_{I,J}\) of
1920	  \cref{def:gr_transition} (a point of \(U^I_J\) and its image under
1921	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) are the two coordinates of a point of the diagonal). We claim
1922	  \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Its image contains all entries of \(X^I\)
1923	  (from the first factor). From the second factor it contains the entries of
1924	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\); taking the \(I\)-minor of this matrix, and using
1925	  \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\), the image contains the entries of \((X^I_J)^{-1}\),
1926	  and in particular
1927	  \[
1928	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = 1/P^I_J .
1929	  \]
1930	  Hence \(1/P^I_J\) lies in the image, so the image is all of
1931	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) and \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Concretely the
1932	  closed subscheme \(\Delta_{I,J} \subseteq U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\) is cut
1933	  out by the entries of the matrix relation
1934	  \[
1935	    X^J_I \, X^I - X^J \;=\; 0,
1936	  \]
1937	  which on the diagonal holds because \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forces
1938	  \(X^J_I = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) and therefore \(X^J_I X^I =
1939	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I = X^J\). As each restricted diagonal is a closed immersion,
1940	  \(\Delta\) is a closed immersion and \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over
1941	  \(\mathbb{Z}\).
1942	\end{proof}
1943	
1944	\section{Properness}
1945	\label{sec:gr_proper}
1946	
1947	\begin{lemma}
1948	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is proper over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
1949	  \label{lem:gr_proper}
1950	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isProper}
1951	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_separated}

FILE: /home/archon/DAG_complexity/calabi_yau_3_orbifold/.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean
/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Patience Ablett, Kevin Buzzard, Harald Carlens, Wayne Ng Kwing King, Michael Schlößer,
  Justus Springer, Andrew Yang, Jujian Zhang
