3279	\end{definition}
3280	\begin{proof}
3281	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3282	  Apply the functorial transport of presentations \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3283	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the step-3 slice equivalence functor
3284	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}\), using
3285	  \cref{def:over_restrict_unit_iso} to identify the image of the structure-sheaf
3286	  (unit) module with the target unit module; this lands a presentation of the
3287	  transported slice module
3288	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)\).
3289	  Finally transport across the pullback packaging isomorphism
3290	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3291	  \cong (U.\iota^{*})\,M\) (\cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) via
3292	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\), giving a presentation of the geometric
3293	  pullback \((U.\iota^{*})\,M\).
3294	\end{proof}

3296	\begin{definition}[Per-cover-member geometric presentation from quasi-coherence data]
3297	  \label{def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}
3298	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData}
3299	  \uses{def:over_restrict_presentation, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}
3300	  \textit{Quasi-coherence data gives a presentation on each cover member's pullback.}
3301	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(X\), let \(q\) be quasi-coherence data for
3302	  \(M\), and let \(i\) be a cover index. There is a presentation of the geometric
3303	  pullback of \(M\) to the open subscheme \(q.X_i\):
3304	  \[
3305	    \bigl((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\bigr).\mathrm{Presentation} .
3306	  \]
3307	\end{definition}
3308	\begin{proof}
3309	  \uses{def:over_restrict_presentation, lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}
3310	  The datum \(q\) packages, for each index \(i\), a slice presentation
3311	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\)
3312	  over the cover member \(q.X_i\) (the per-member presentations whose binding is
3313	  recorded by \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData}\), \cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}).
3314	  Feeding this slice presentation to \cref{def:over_restrict_presentation} at
3315	  \(U = q.X_i\) produces the desired presentation of the geometric pullback
3316	  \((q.X_i).\iota^{*}\,M\).
3317	\end{proof}

3321	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover (gap1, P1)]
3322	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
3323	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
3324	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3325	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3326	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3327	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
3328	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen does NOT yet exist; it is the
3329	  % per-element transport step of gap1. It replaces the former lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover,
3330	  % whose \lean{} pin named a non-existent decl (and whose Mayer--Vietoris framing is superseded by
3331	  % the section-localization descent of lem:section_localization_descent).
3332	  \textit{Per-element step: on each basic open of the refined cover, the restricted sheaf is a
3333	  geometric tilde.}
3334	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data \(q\), and let \(r \in R\)
3335	  be such that \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for some member of the cover (as produced by
3336	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). Then the restricted counit is an
3337	  isomorphism,
3338	  \[
3339	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr),
3340	  \]
3341	  i.e. \(M|_{D(r)}\) is identified with the tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) of the \(R_r\)-module
3342	  \(N = \Gamma(M, D(r))\) on \(\Spec R_r \cong D(r)\).
3343	\end{lemma}
3344	\begin{proof}
3345	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3346	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3347	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3348	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData,
3349	    def:over_restrict_presentation, def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}
3350	  The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
3351	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\) on the slice over
3352	  \(q.X_i\). Restrict it along the inclusion of opens \(D(r) \subseteq q.X_i\) by transporting across
3353	  the iterated Grothendieck slice — the template is \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData.bind}\)
3354	  (\cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}) — using \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3355	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the colimit-preserving slice functor; this yields a
3356	  presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(D(r))\). Pushing through the slice-to-geometric bridge
3357	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso} converts it into a presentation of the geometric restriction
3358	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,(D(r)).\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\); then, across the affine
3359	  identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, by
3360	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) along the pullback functor \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), into
3361	  a genuine global presentation of \(M|_{D(r)}\) as a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R_r\). A global
3362	  presentation forces the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism
3363	  (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib}), giving
3364	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr)\).
3365	\end{proof}

3398	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}

3400	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3401	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3402	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3403	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction

3405	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3406	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).

3417	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3418	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3419	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3420	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3421	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3422	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)

This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.

The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
transport of the previous section:

1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).

Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
is project-local. -/

section BasicOpenPresentationDescent

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).

For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.

It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
      (SheafOfModules.pushforward (𝟙 ((q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))

end Scheme.Modules

end BasicOpenPresentationDescent

end AlgebraicGeometry

end Scheme.Modules

end SliceGeometricPresentation

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _

instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
  intro M
  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',

  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective

/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=

    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])

/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
is final. -/
noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)

/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).

For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
of schemes is `Final`. This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `Presentation.map`
along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf :=
  asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))

end Scheme.Modules

end BasicOpenPresentationDescent

/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).

For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
of schemes is `Final`. This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `Presentation.map`
along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf :=
  asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
input.new_string:
/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapId _

/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` instance needed by
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Project-local. -/
instance opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
  infer_instance

/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).

Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:
applied with `φ` the `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` of the affine restriction, it moves the presentation onto
a genuine `Spec`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z)
    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ)

end Scheme.Modules

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).

For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
a geometric tilde.

This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).

Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is
`Spec R_r` since `D(r)` is affine) — has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit, i.e. `M|_{D(r)}` is a
geometric tilde.

This is the per-element step of gap1: it is the affine instance `W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` of
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, with affineness of `W` from
`IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen` (`D(r)` is affine in `Spec R`) and
`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (its preimage under the open immersion `(q.X i).ι` is
affine, using `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`). Project-local. -/
theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (r : R) (i : q.I)
    (hr : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
        (((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
          (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr)).isoSpec.inv)).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι
          ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
  isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback M q i
    ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r))
    ((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
      (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr))

end Scheme.Modules

     equivalence needed** because `PN` is already a global presentation on the scheme `Z`. This is the

  3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` geometrizes to a presentation of `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on
     `W.toScheme` (reused this iter's lemma). → `presentationPullbackιRestrict`.
  4. `W` affine (= preimage of `D(r)`); `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec : W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z,W)`; transport

     unit-iso needs `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)`, which holds because the iso's `Opens.map` is
     `Final`. → `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
